title: Dose Escalation and Dose Expansion Study of RMC-6291 Monotherapy in Subjects With Advanced KRASG12C Mutant Solid Tumors

eligibility.text: Subject must be ≥18 years of age.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the trial can recruit participants within a specific age range.

eligibility.text: Subject must have pathologically documented, locally advanced or metastatic KRASG12C-mutated solid tumor malignancy (not amenable to curative surgery) that has previously been treated with standard-of-care therapies for respective tumor types, is intolerant to, or is considered ineligible for standard-of-care anticancer treatments.
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures the study population is representative of a specific patient group likely to benefit from treatment.
This criterion ensures patients are able to tolerate and participate in the trial.

eligibility.text: Prior treatment with a KRASG12C (OFF) inhibitor allowed for dose escalation and for NSCLC in dose expansion
eligibility.justification: This criterion ensures patients have received prior treatment, allowing for comparison of the new treatment to previous therapies.
This criterion ensures patients are healthy enough to tolerate the trial's interventions.
This criterion limits the study population, potentially impacting recruitment and data quality.
This criterion ensures patients are healthy enough to tolerate the trial's interventions.

eligibility.text: Known impairment of GI function that would alter the absorption
eligibility.justification: This criterion limits the study population, potentially impacting recruitment and data quality.

eligibility.text: Major surgical procedures within 28 days or non-study-related minor procedures within 7 days of treatment.
eligibility.justification: This criterion limits the study population, potentially impacting recruitment and data quality.
This criterion ensures patients are healthy enough to tolerate the trial's interventions.
This criterion allows for flexibility in recruitment and data quality.

power_analysis.assessment: N/A — Phase 1 trial, not powered for efficacy

endpoints.text: Maximum Observed Blood Concentration of RMC-6291
Time to Reach Maximum Blood Concentration of RMC-6291
Area Under Blood Concentration Time Curve of RMC-6291
Ratio of accumulation of RMC-6291 from a single dose to steady state with repeated dosing

randomization.randomization_description: Stratified by Center, Prior Treatment, Age.

adaptive_designs.description: Adaptive design detected: Types identified: Dose-Finding / Phase I.

safety_adverse_events.ae_ascertainment: Not explicitly described (assumed standard investigator reporting)
safety_adverse_events.safety_monitoring: Investigator-reported with sponsor oversight (no DSMB mentioned)

known_effects: Treatment response - KRAS G12C mutation required

pharmacogenetics.genetic_screen_failure_summary: Detected 1 genetic biomarker requirement(s) in eligibility: KRAS_G12C (pop. prevalence ~13%, ~87% screen fail). Worst-case screen failure rate from genetic criteria: ~87%.
pharmacogenetics.summary: Genes detected (KRAS) but could not map indication "non-small cell lung cancer" to a GWAS EFO trait. Try alternative trait name.

relaxations.description: Decrease minimum age from 18 to 13 years
relaxations.raw_text: * Subject must be ≥18 years of age.
